notes: Scan contains TWO copies of the same log. Pages 1-2: 'Record of Well (Shaft or Bore)' form, feet+inches columns, O.D. 330 ft, date of well 1909, NGR:- NZ 2606 4311 in margin. Pages 3-4: 'Record of Shaft or Bore for Minerals' form, native fathoms/feet/inches columns, 'Level at which bore commenced relative to O.D. cc 320'', date of sinking Aug. 1909, made by Wm. Coulson Ltd for Messrs. Framwellgate Coal & Coke Co, information from Wm. Coulson Ltd. Bk 11 p 156, date received July 1941; exact site '3 yds from W. hedge & 63 yds from S. hedge in the hilly field which lies on the E. side of the Newcastle Road & opposite Crofton Holme, Crossgate Moor'. The fathoms copy was transcribed as primary (native ffi columns, richer water remarks, splits the composite coal entries); every interval cross-checked against the feet copy — running depths agree at every row and the arithmetic chain of the fathoms copy is clean end to end (previous depth + thickness = depth for all 41 intervals). Disagreements between the copies: (1) surface datum 330 ft (well form) vs cc 320' (minerals form) — minerals-form value recorded, flag for downstream; (2) well copy writes depth 234-0 where arithmetic and the fathoms copy give 239-0 (interval 25) — slip on that copy only; (3) interval 22 'Lost water at 218'6"' (minerals) vs 'little water at depth 218'6' (well copy); (4) well copy lacks 'Water still getting away' at interval 24; (5) well copy combines the Brass Thill trio and the Hutton pair into single composite entries (2-8 at 241-8 and 3-9 at 291-4) — totals agree with the split rows used here. Margin of p3 Geological Classification column carries 'Drift' against the drift sequence (208 ft of drift — deep buried-valley fill). Parish word on p1 header is hard to read (Fernhill?); not log content. Final depth 293 ft = 89.31 m, matching the BGS index (89.31).
